evidence_quotes.quote: The addictive design features is a real problem on the videogames side and I'm very happy that is being addressed in this initiative.

paragraphs.text: The addictive design features is a real problem on the videogames side and I'm very happy that is being addressed in this initiative. Another practice that is not included and would fit here is the inability to play videogames that you already paid for, and have some form of online check, but publishers decided that it was not profitable anymore to maintain those servers. This is a problem that is been appearing recently a lot and already has happened to hundreds of games. Take the example of Ubisoft's Videogame The Crew. This game was perfectly playable without any need of multiplayer or online services but Ubisoft closed the servers last year so now you cannot play the game anymore ever again, even though you payed for it. Early next year Electronic Arts "Anthem" is going to be erased for ever. This game has an optional online cooperative component alongside playing the videogame solo but because of the "always online" check you need to be connected to the internet to play. What this means is that in January 2026 when EA shuts down the servers I will not be able to play the game I payed for anymore because of this issue. This is only the start of this problem as more and more games are requiring you to be always online to play although it may serve no need for the actual gameplay. Some example of more recent released games that I love (and payed for) and will suffer this in the future: Fallout 76 Temtem Diablo 4 Read Dead Redemption 2 Hitman 3 I have played all of these games alone and not being able to do so in the future it's not ideal. Thanks for the chance of making this issue being heard.
